goals: Make the "define requirement" phase interactive for resolving open questions.
Address any generated open questions one-by-one with the user.
Provide the user with recommendations/suggestions for answering the open questions.

userStories.title: Agent asks open questions interactively
userStories.description: As a user, I want the agent to ask me any open questions interactively at the end of the PRD generation so that no requirements are left ambiguous.

acceptanceCriteria.text: At the end of the PRD generation, the agent detects if there are any open questions.
The agent asks the open questions to the user one by one.
The agent waits for the user's answer before proceeding to the next question.

userStories.title: Agent provides suggestions for open questions
userStories.description: As a user, I want the agent to provide suggestions or recommendations when asking open questions so that I have helpful context to provide an answer.

acceptanceCriteria.text: When an open question is asked, the agent includes valid suggestions or inferred options for the user to choose from.

userStories.title: Sync agent skills definition
userStories.description: As an internal process, I want the skill modifications to happen in both `.agents/` and `scaffold/` directories so that standard project context remains consistent across instantiated and scaffolded projects.

acceptanceCriteria.text: The prompt/skill files updated (e.g., `create-pr-document.md`) are modified identically in both `.agents/skills/` and `scaffold/agents/skills/`.

functionalRequirements.description: Update the skill prompt for PRD generation (`create-pr-document` or similar) to instruct the agent to append an interactive Q&A session if "Open Questions" exist after drafting the PRD.
Ensure the skill modifications are mirrored between the working directory (`.agents/skills/`) and the scaffold directory (`scaffold/agents/skills/`).
The interactive workflow must process questions sequentially, one at a time, not parallel.
